questions: What was your primary reasoning approach?
Which BSKG queries were most informative?
What hypothesis did you form and how did you validate it?
answers: I followed graph-first methodology: built the knowledge graph first, then queried for behavioral patterns (external calls before state writes, access control). The BSKG revealed the R:bal -> X:out -> W:bal pattern before I read any source code.

The query for functions where external call precedes state write was most informative. It directly identified the reentrancy pattern using semantic operations (TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT before WRITES_USER_BALANCE) rather than relying on function names.

My hypothesis was that F-SimpleVault-withdraw contains a reentrancy vulnerability based on the operation ordering detected by BSKG. I validated this by: (1) confirming the R:bal -> X:out -> W:bal pattern in the graph, (2) checking for absence of reentrancy guard modifiers, (3) reading source code to confirm the external call precedes the state update.
